questions.question: Sentences of a paragraph are given below in jumbled order. Arrange the sentences in the correct order to form a meaningful and coherent paragraph. A. He had a tiny room in a basement, the one window of which looked out on to the street. B. There was hardly a pair of boots in the neighbourhood that had not been once or twice through his hands. C. In a certain town there lived a cobbler, Martin Avdeitch by name. D. Through it one could see the feet of those who passed by, but Martin recognised the people by their boots.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is 'Option 2' i.e. 'CADB'. ![](https://cdn.testbook.com/resources/lms_creative_elements/key-point-image.png) Key Point • C. In a certain town there lived a cobbler, Martin Avdeitch by name. • The paragraph begins by introducing Martin Avdeitch, a cobbler who lived in a specific town. • A. He had a tiny room in a basement, the one window of which looked out onto the street. • This sentence provides information about Martin's living situation. It mentions that he resided in a small room located in a basement, and the only window in his room faced the street. • D. Through it one could see the feet of those who passed by, but Martin recognized the people by their boots. • The next sentence builds on the previous one, referring to the window in Martin's room. It explains that Martin would observe the feet of people passing by through that window, and he could recognize individuals by the boots they wore. • B. There was hardly a pair of boots in the neighborhood that had not been once or twice through his hands. • This sentence provides further information about Martin's role as a cobbler. It states that nearly every pair of boots in the neighborhood had been repaired or handled by him at least once. • By arranging the sentences in the order CADB, the paragraph presents a coherent and logical progression of information. • It starts with the introduction of Martin, describes his living conditions, explains his ability to recognize people by their boots, and concludes by highlighting his extensive involvement as a cobbler in the neighborhood. Therefore, the correct answer is: 'CADB'

questions.question: The following sentence has been divided into parts. One of them may contain a spelling error. Select the part that contains the error from the given options. If you don’t find any error, mark ‘No error’ as your answer. The state’s human development performance, / including on such measures as life expactency and female literacy, / is not just middling but downright bad.
options: is not just middling but downright bad
including on such measures as life expactency and female literacy
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 4. ![](https://cdn.testbook.com/resources/lms_creative_elements/key-point-image.png) Key Points • The primary issue in the sentence is the spelling error in the word "expactency". • The correct spelling is "expectancy". • Thus, the error is located in part 4 of the sentence. Therefore, the correct answer is Option 4. Correct Sentence: "The state’s human development performance, including on such measures as life expectancy and female literacy, is not just middling but downright bad." ![](https://cdn.testbook.com/resources/lms_creative_elements/additional-information-image.png) Additional Information • 1: "is not just middling but downright bad," – This part of the sentence is grammatically correct and does not require any changes. • 3: "The state’s human development performance," – This part of the sentence is grammatically correct and does not require any changes. • 2: "No error" – This would be the correct option if there were no mistakes in the sentence, but there is a spelling error in part 4.

questions.question: Fill in the blank with an appropriate option. No matter how many times I try, I always end up in the same ______.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is 'Option 3' i.e. 'Pickle'. ![](https://cdn.testbook.com/resources/lms_creative_elements/key-point-image.png) Key Points • The word "pickle" in this context is used metaphorically to mean a difficult or unfavorable situation or predicament. • When someone says they always end up in the same pickle, they mean they consistently find themselves in a challenging or undesirable circumstance, regardless of their efforts. Therefore, the correct sentence is: No matter how many times I try, I always end up in the same pickle. ![](https://cdn.testbook.com/resources/lms_creative_elements/additional-information-image.png) Additional Information Account: In this context, "account" refers to a financial record or narrative of events. Meal: In this context, "meal" refers to a prepared or cooked food. Sauce: In this context, "sauce" refers to a liquid or semi-liquid condiment or accompaniment to food.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank. The students arranged an open _______ on the topic of pandemics and social life after them.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is "Option 4: debate". ![](https://cdn.testbook.com/resources/lms_creative_elements/key-point-image.png) Key Points • The word "debate" refers to a formal discussion on a particular topic where opposing arguments are put forward, fitting perfectly within the context of the sentence. • An "open debate" implies that different or opposing views were allowed and encouraged, which aligns with the context of a discussion on pandemics. • None of the other options make sense in the context of the sentence. Therefore, the correct answer is "Option 4: debate". ![](https://cdn.testbook.com/resources/lms_creative_elements/additional-information-image.png) Additional Information • An "open drought" doesn't make sense in relation to arranging an event by students. • "Open debase" has no clear meaning, and the term 'debase' typically implies the action of reducing something in quality or value. • "Open debt" would not make sense contextually as it isn't an event that can be arranged.

questions.question: Rearrange the parts of the sentence in correct order. P. more stringent mobility control and physical distancing policies Q. East Asian governments (except for Japan) implemented R. as well as more comprehensive testing, tracing S. and isolation policies since the early stages
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: QPRS. ![](https://cdn.testbook.com/resources/lms_creative_elements/key-point-image.png) Key Points • Let's look at the correct sequence of the parts of the sentence: • East Asian governments (except for Japan) implemented • more stringent mobility control and physical distancing policies • as well as more comprehensive testing, tracing • and isolation policies since the early stages. • The sentence throws light on the fact that since the beginning, East Asian governments (excluding Japan) have put in place stricter rules to control people's movements and maintain physical distance. They have also implemented more thorough policies for testing, tracing, and isolating individuals to effectively manage the situation. Therefore, the correct answer is: QPRS.

questions.question: Select the correct direct form of the given sentence. He asked Rima if she spoke Spanish fluently.
options: He said, "Rima, does you speak Spanish fluently?"
He asked, "Do you speak Spanish fluently?"
He said to Rima, "Do you speak Spanish fluently?"
He told Rima, "Will you speak Spanish fluently?"
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3) i.e. He said to Rima, "Do you speak Spanish fluently?" ![](https://cdn.testbook.com/resources/lms_creative_elements/key-point-image.png) Key Points • The given sentence is in Indirect Speech. As per the given directions, we have to change it into Direct Speech. • As we have to transform the given sentence into an interrogative sentence, the process of transformation is as follows: • The conjunction 'if' will be removed and comma and inverted commas will be added. • In the direct speech 'asked' will be changed into 'said to' because reporting verb has an object, 'Rima.' • The direct speech will begin like 'He said to Rima​.' • Now, let's focus on the reported speech. • The pronoun 'she' will be changed into 'you.' • The simple past tense 'spoke' will be changed into the simple present tense 'speak.' • Here, 'she spoke Spanish fluently' is an assertive sentence and will be changed into an interrogative sentence i.e., 'Do you speak Spanish fluently?' • Finally, the result is: He said to Rima, "Do you speak Spanish fluently?" • ​So, the correct answer is option 3. Correct Sentence: He said to Rima, "Do you speak Spanish fluently?"

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option that can substitute the underlined segment in the given sentence. The volunteers of the organisation refused point blank of do what was requested.
options: refused in point blank of doing
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: refused point blank to do. ![](https://cdn.testbook.com/resources/lms_creative_elements/key-point-image.png) Key Points • The phrase "refused point blank to do" correctly follows the structure for expressing an outright refusal to perform an action. • "Point blank" is an adverbial phrase meaning 'directly' or 'straightforwardly,' and it properly pairs with "to do" to indicate refusal without hesitation or explanation. • The use of "to do" after "point blank" is grammatically correct, as it directly links the refusal with the infinitive form of the verb that describes the action being refused. • This option maintains clarity and conciseness while accurately conveying the intended meaning of an outright refusal. Therefore, the correct answer is: The volunteers of the organisation refused point blank to do what was requested.

questions.question: Direction: Select the most appropriate option to substitute the bracketed segment in the given sentence. If there is no need to substitute it, select 'no substitution required'. (Beside myself, the only English people) there were Keith and Doreen.
options: Besides I, the only English speaking people
Besides me, the only English people
questions.explanation: The correct answer is '4' i.e. Besides me, the only English people . ![](https://cdn.testbook.com/resources/lms_creative_elements/key-point-image.png) Key Points • "Besides me" – The use of "Besides" is correct because it means "apart from" or "in addition to", which fits the context of the sentence. • "The only English people there were Keith and Doreen." – This part of the sentence is grammatically sound, as it correctly identifies the subject ("the only English people") and links it to Keith and Doreen using the verb "were". Hence, the correct answer is: Besides me, the only English people.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank. Shraddha wandered through the labyrinth of thoughts, seeking clarity _____ chaos.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Option 4. ![](https://cdn.testbook.com/resources/lms_creative_elements/key-point-image.png) Key Points • The word "amidst" means being surrounded by or in the middle of something. (के बीच में) • Example: She found herself amidst a crowd of enthusiastic supporters. • "Amidst" refers to a position in the middle of or surrounded by something, often used to describe being in the middle of a situation or environment. (बीच में) • Example: The company thrived amidst economic uncertainty. • Hence, we can infer that the correct word to convey the meaning of seeking clarity in the middle of chaos is 'amidst'. Therefore, the correct answer is: amidst. ![](https://cdn.testbook.com/resources/lms_creative_elements/additional-information-image.png) Additional Information Here are the other options explained along with their Hindi meanings and example sentences: • for (के लिए): Indicates purpose or intention. • Example: I am looking for a new job. • upstairs (ऊपर): Situated on an upper floor. • Example: The bedroom is upstairs. • down (नीचे): Towards a lower place or position. • Example: She walked down the stairs.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate antonym of the given word: BUOYANT
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Gloomy. ![](https://cdn.testbook.com/resources/lms_creative_elements/key-point-image.png) Key Points • Let's look at the meanings of the given word and marked option: • Buoyant- cheerful and optimistic (हंसमुख और आशावादी) • Gloomy- feeling distressed or pessimistic (व्यथित या निराशावादी महसूस करना) • ​​Let's look at the meanings of the other given options: • Cheerful- noticeably happy and optimistic (उल्लेखनीय रूप से खुश और आशावादी) • Neutral- not helping or supporting either side in a conflict, disagreement (संघर्ष, असहमति में किसी भी पक्ष की मदद या समर्थन नहीं करना) • Bouncy- bouncing or causing things to bounce ( ) • Hence from the given meanings, we find that Buoyant and Gloomy are antonyms.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word. DETERIORATE
questions.explanation: The correct answer is 'Worsen'. ![](https://cdn.testbook.com/resources/lms_creative_elements/key-point-image.png) Key Points • ​The word Deteriorate(verb) means- "to become worse". (बदतर हो जाना) • E.g: My eyesight began to deteriorate quite rapidly.​ • ​The synonyms of the given word Deteriorate are- 'Worsen', 'decline', 'decay', 'fall', 'decompose', etc. • From the synonyms of the given word, we can say that the word 'Worsen' is similar in meaning. • The word Worsen(verb) means- "to become worse or to make something become worse". (बदतर होना या कुछ खराब करना) • E.g: The continued supply of arms to the region will only worsen the situation.​ • ​Hence, the correct option is Option 2). • Let's have a look at the meaning of other given options: • Enliven(verb)- "to make something more interesting". (कुछ और दिलचस्प बनाने के लिए) • Fasten(verb)- "to make or become firmly attached or closed". (मजबूती से जुड़ा या बंद होना या बनना) • Improve(verb)- "to get better, or to make something better". (बेहतर करने के लिए, या कुछ बेहतर करने के लिए) ![](https://cdn.testbook.com/resources/lms_creative_elements/additional-information-image.png) Additional Information • The antonyms of the given word Deteriorate are- 'Improve', 'better', 'upgrade', 'develop', etc. • Example of 'Deteriorate' in a sentence: • The political situation in the region has deteriorated rapidly.

questions.question: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in passive voice. Rover would not swallow the pill.
options: The pill would not be swallowing by Rover.
The pill would not swallowed by Rover.
The pill would not be swallowed by Rover.
The pill would not be swallow by Rover.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is 'The pill would not be swallowed by Rover.' ![](https://cdn.testbook.com/resources/lms_creative_elements/key-point-image.png) Key Points • The given sentence is an example of Active Voice. As per the given question, we have to change it to Passive Voice. • The process of transformation is as follows: • The subject of the given sentence is 'Rover'. • The object of the given sentence is 'the pill'. • Since a modal verb has been used in the active voice, therefore, the passive voice will follow the given structure:- • Active Voice - Subject + modal verb(would) + V1 + Object. • Passive Voice - Object(Subjective form) modal verb(would) + be + V3 Subject (Objective form). • Here, 'would not swallow' is changed into 'would not be swallowed' because in the 'active to passive' transformation third form of the verb is used. • Therefore, as per the above-mentioned points, we find that the correct answer is Option 3. ​ Correct Sentence: The pill would not be swallowed by Rover.

questions.question: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in passive voice. Does Sneha not lick the butter?
options: Is the butter being licked by Sneha?
Is the butter not licked by Sneha?
Sneha does not lick the butter?
Was Sneha not licking the butter?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is 'Is the butter not licked by Sneha?' ![](https://cdn.testbook.com/resources/lms_creative_elements/key-point-image.png) Key Points • The given sentence is written in an interrogative form as the question tag is there, and we need to convert it into the passive form. • Active Voice describes a sentence where the subject performs the action stated by the verb. While in the Passive Voice subject is acted upon by the verb. • We follow the following structure in case of an interrogative sentence: • Active voice: Do/Does/Did + Doer + Verb 1 + Noun/Pronoun + Rest of the sentence • Example - Do they beat you every day? (Active) • Passive voice: Am/is/are/was/were + Noun/Pronoun + Verb 3 + By + Doer + Rest of the sentence • Example - Are you beaten by them every day? (Passive) • While converting Interrogative Active into Interrogative Passive Voice. • The helping verb comes before the object in the passive voice, (as the passive voice starts with the object of the active voice) • Convert the verb into past participle or 3rd form of the verb. • Use the preposition ‘by’. • Complete the sentence with the subject and sign of Interrogation (?). • Thus, the correct answer is Option 2. Correct Passive form: Is the butter not licked by Sneha?

questions.question: Directions: Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word. INTEGRATE
questions.explanation: The correct answer is "Unite". ![](https://cdn.testbook.com/resources/lms_creative_elements/key-point-image.png) Key Points • The word 'Integrate' means to combine (one thing) with another to form a whole. ((एक चीज) को दूसरे के साथ मिलाकर एक संपूर्ण बनाना) • The synonyms of the word 'Integrate' are "Unite, Join, Link". • From the synonym of the given word, we can say that the word 'Unite' has the same meaning. • The word 'Unite' means to come or bring together to form a whole. Hence, the correct answer is option 4. Let's see the meaning of other given options- WORDS MEANING Divide separate or be separated into parts (अलग या भागों में अलग होना) Increase become or make greater in size, amount, or degree (आकार, राशि या डिग्री में बड़ा होना या बनना) Disappear cease to be visible (दिखना बंद) ![](https://cdn.testbook.com/resources/lms_creative_elements/additional-information-image.png) Additional Information • The antonyms of the word 'Integrate' are "disunite, divide, separate". • Example of 'Integrate' in a sentence: • We should integrate these systems and learn from each other.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate one-word substitution for the given group of words. Someone who avoids other people's company.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is 'Option 3' i.e. 'Reclusive'. ![](https://cdn.testbook.com/resources/lms_creative_elements/key-point-image.png) Key Points • The group of words "Someone who avoids other people's company" can be substituted with the word "Reclusive". • "Reclusive" refers to a person who lives in voluntary seclusion from the public and society.(वैरागी) Therefore, the correct answer is: 'Reclusive'. ![](https://cdn.testbook.com/resources/lms_creative_elements/additional-information-image.png) Additional Information • Let's examine the other options: • Amiable: This refers to a person who is friendly and pleasant.(मिलनसार) • Gregarious: This refers to a person who enjoys being in crowds and socializing(सामूहिक) • Amicable: This refers to having a disposition characterized by warmth and friendliness.(मिलनसार)

questions.question: Select the INCORRECTLY spelt word.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 2) ![](https://cdn.testbook.com/resources/lms_creative_elements/key-point-image.png) Key Points • The word "Priviledge" is incorrectly spelled. The correct spelling is "Privilege". (विशेषाधिकार) • Example: Having access to quality education is a privilege that not everyone enjoys. • "Privilege" refers to a special right, advantage, or immunity granted or available only to a particular person or group. (विशेषाधिकार) • Example: It was a privilege to witness such a historic event. Therefore, the correct spelling is Privilege. ![](https://cdn.testbook.com/resources/lms_creative_elements/additional-information-image.png) Additional Information Here are the other options explained along with their Hindi meanings and example sentences: • Recommendation (सिफारिश): A suggestion or proposal as to the best course of action, especially one put forward by an authoritative body. • Example: She gave me a recommendation letter for my job application. • Affiliate (संबद्ध): Officially attach or connect (a subsidiary group or a person) to an organization. • Example: The local club is affiliated with the national organization. • Parachute (पैराशूट): A device used to slow the descent of a person or object through the air. • Example: He jumped out of the plane and deployed his parachute..

questions.question: The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains an error. Neetu have been / waiting for me / since 10 o’clock / in the morning.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is "Neetu have been." ![](https://cdn.testbook.com/resources/lms_creative_elements/key-point-image.png) Key Points • In the given sentence, the error lies in the first part. • The subject "Neetu" is singular, but the verb "have been" is plural. • This creates a subject-verb agreement error. • The correct verb form for "Neetu" would be "has been." • The sentence should read: "Neetu has been waiting for me since 10 o'clock in the morning." Hence, the correct answer is option 1.

questions.question: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in indirect speech. The doctor said to the patient, "Take two tablets after every meal."
options: The doctor asked the patient to taking two tablets after every meal.
The doctor told the patient to took two tablets after every meal.
The doctor advised the patient to take two tablets after every meal.
The doctor advised the patient to take two tablets before each meal.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3) i.e. 'The doctor advised the patient to take two tablets after every meal.' ![](https://cdn.testbook.com/resources/lms_creative_elements/key-point-image.png) Key Points • The given sentence is an imperative sentence in Direct Speech. As per the given directions, we have to change it into Indirect Speech. • The process of transformation is as follows: • The conjunction 'to' will be added and comma and inverted commas will be removed. • In the Indirect speech 'said to' will be changed into 'advised' because the doctor is giving advice to the patient. • The indirect speech will begin like 'The doctor advised the patient to.' • Now we have to change the reported speech. • The imperative sentence will be changed into an assertive sentence. • According to the rules of grammar, after 'to' we have to use 'V1.' • Here, no other changes are required in the reported speech. • Finally, the indirect speech is 'The doctor advised the patient to take two tablets after every meal.' • So, the correct answer is option 3. Correct Sentence: The doctor advised the patient to take two tablets after every meal.

questions.question: Choose the word that can substitute the given group of words. A lover of oneself, of one’s advancement
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Egoist. ![](https://cdn.testbook.com/resources/lms_creative_elements/key-point-image.png) Key Points • The word "egoist" is the correct option as it represents a person who is excessively self-centered or self-focused. • An egoist is someone who loves and prioritizes oneself and is primarily concerned with personal advancement and well-being. Therefore, the correct answer is: Egoist. ![](https://cdn.testbook.com/resources/lms_creative_elements/additional-information-image.png) Additional Information • The meanings of the rest of the options are given below: • Philistine: Philistine refers to a person who is indifferent or hostile to artistic, intellectual, or cultural values and pursuits. In Hindi: ग्रामीण • Mercenary: Mercenary describes a person who is motivated primarily by personal gain or profit, often willing to do anything for money or material rewards. In Hindi: प्रवासी सैनिक • Cynic: Cynic refers to a person who distrusts or doubts the motives and sincerity of others, often having a pessimistic or negative outlook on life. In Hindi: नास्तिक

questions.question: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word. Altercation
questions.explanation: The correct answer is "Agreement" ![](https://cdn.testbook.com/resources/lms_creative_elements/key-point-image.png) Key Points • Let's see the meanings of the given words- • Altercation- a noisy argument or disagreement, especially in public. • Quarrel- a heated argument or disagreement, typically about a trivial issue and between people who are usually on good terms. • Agreement- harmony or accordance in opinion or feeling. • Controversy- a prolonged public disagreement or heated discussion. • Argument- an exchange of diverging or opposite views, typically a heated or angry one • Hence, according to the meanings of the given words, the correct answer is Option 2 i.e. Agreement. ![](https://cdn.testbook.com/resources/lms_creative_elements/hinglish-image.png) Hinglish • आइए देखें दिए गए शब्दों के अर्थ- • Altercation- एक शोर तर्क या असहमति, विशेष रूप से सार्वजनिक रूप से। • Quarrel- एक गर्म तर्क या असहमति, आमतौर पर एक तुच्छ मुद्दे के बारे में और उन लोगों के बीच जो आमतौर पर अच्छी शर्तों पर होते हैं। • Agreement- सद्भाव या राय या भावना के अनुसार। • Controversy- एक लंबे समय तक सार्वजनिक असहमति या गर्म चर्चा। • Argument- अलग-अलग या विपरीत विचारों का आदान-प्रदान, आमतौर पर एक गर्म या गुस्से में एक

questions.question: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. Once, there was a boy who became bored when he watched over the village sheep (1)________ on the hillside. To entertain himself, he sang out, “Wolf! Wolf! The wolf is chasing the sheep!” When the villagers heard the cry, they came (2)________ the hill to drive the wolf away. But, when they arrived, they saw no wolf. The boy was amused when seeing their angry faces. “Don’t scream wolf, boy,” warned the villagers, “when there is no wolf!” They angrily went back down the hill. Later, the shepherd boy cried out once again, “Wolf! Wolf! The wolf is chasing the sheep!” When the villagers came, they saw there was no wolf, they said strictly, “Save your (3)________ cry for when there is really a wolf! Don’t cry ‘wolf’ when there is no wolf!” Later, the boy saw a real wolf sneaking around his flock. Alarmed, he jumped on his feet and cried out as loud as he could, “Wolf! Wolf!” But the villagers thought he was (4)________ them again, and so they didn’t come to help. At sunset, the villagers went looking for the boy who hadn’t returned with their sheep. When they went up the hill, they found him (5)________ as all the sheep were dead. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 1.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is 'grazing'. ![](https://cdn.testbook.com/resources/lms_creative_elements/key-point-image.png) Key Points • In the context of the passage, the boy is watching over the sheep on the hillside. This suggests that the sheep are engaged in a common sheep activity like eating grass. • "Grazing" is the term commonly used to describe sheep eating grass in a field or on a hillside. (चरते हुए) • The other options ("laughing," "receiving," "assisting") do not make logical sense in the given context as they do not describe typical sheep behavior. • "Grazing" fits logically and contextually, describing what the sheep are doing while the boy is watching them. Therefore, the correct answer is 'Option 4'. Complete Passage: Once, there was a boy who became bored when he watched over the village sheep grazing on the hillside. To entertain himself, he sang out, “Wolf! Wolf! The wolf is chasing the sheep!” When the villagers heard the cry, they came (2)________ the hill to drive the wolf away. But, when they arrived, they saw no wolf. The boy was amused when seeing their angry faces. “Don’t scream wolf, boy,” warned the villagers, “when there is no wolf!” They angrily went back down the hill. Later, the shepherd boy cried out once again, “Wolf! Wolf! The wolf is chasing the sheep!” When the villagers came, they saw there was no wolf, they said strictly, “Save your (3)________ cry for when there is really a wolf! Don’t cry ‘wolf’ when there is no wolf!” Later, the boy saw a real wolf sneaking around his flock. Alarmed, he jumped on his feet and cried out as loud as he could, “Wolf! Wolf!” But the villagers thought he was (4)________ them again, and so they didn’t come to help. At sunset, the villagers went looking for the boy who hadn’t returned with their sheep. When they went up the hill, they found him (5)________ as all the sheep were dead. ![](https://cdn.testbook.com/resources/lms_creative_elements/additional-information-image.png) Additional Information • Laughing (हंसते हुए): Sheep do not laugh; "laughing" (हंसते हुए) is a behaviour attributed to humans or, in some cases, other animals, but not to sheep in this context. • Receiving (प्राप्त करते हुए): "Receiving" (प्राप्त करते हुए) implies that the sheep are being given something, which does not align with the context of the boy watching them on a hillside. • Assisting (मदद करते हुए): "Assisting" (मदद करते हुए) suggests that the sheep are helping with some task, which is not logical in the context where the boy is simply watching them.

questions.question: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. Once, there was a boy who became bored when he watched over the village sheep (1)________ on the hillside. To entertain himself, he sang out, “Wolf! Wolf! The wolf is chasing the sheep!” When the villagers heard the cry, they came (2)________ the hill to drive the wolf away. But, when they arrived, they saw no wolf. The boy was amused when seeing their angry faces. “Don’t scream wolf, boy,” warned the villagers, “when there is no wolf!” They angrily went back down the hill. Later, the shepherd boy cried out once again, “Wolf! Wolf! The wolf is chasing the sheep!” When the villagers came, they saw there was no wolf, they said strictly, “Save your (3)________ cry for when there is really a wolf! Don’t cry ‘wolf’ when there is no wolf!” Later, the boy saw a real wolf sneaking around his flock. Alarmed, he jumped on his feet and cried out as loud as he could, “Wolf! Wolf!” But the villagers thought he was (4)________ them again, and so they didn’t come to help. At sunset, the villagers went looking for the boy who hadn’t returned with their sheep. When they went up the hill, they found him (5)________ as all the sheep were dead. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 2.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is 'running up'. ![](https://cdn.testbook.com/resources/lms_creative_elements/key-point-image.png) Key Points • The context involves villagers responding urgently to the boy's cry for help. • The boy is on a hill, so villagers must move upward to reach him. • The phrase "running up" indicates both the urgency and the direction of motion needed to reach the boy. • Other options ("driving," "walking down," "running down") do not fit the scenario of moving towards the boy on the hill. Therefore, the correct answer is 'Option 4'. Complete Passage: Once, there was a boy who became bored when he watched over the village sheep grazing on the hillside. To entertain himself, he sang out, “Wolf! Wolf! The wolf is chasing the sheep!” When the villagers heard the cry, they came running up the hill to drive the wolf away. But, when they arrived, they saw no wolf. The boy was amused when seeing their angry faces. “Don’t scream wolf, boy,” warned the villagers, “when there is no wolf!” They angrily went back down the hill. Later, the shepherd boy cried out once again, “Wolf! Wolf! The wolf is chasing the sheep!” When the villagers came, they saw there was no wolf, they said strictly, “Save your (3)________ cry for when there is really a wolf! Don’t cry ‘wolf’ when there is no wolf!” Later, the boy saw a real wolf sneaking around his flock. Alarmed, he jumped on his feet and cried out as loud as he could, “Wolf! Wolf!” But the villagers thought he was (4)________ them again, and so they didn’t come to help. At sunset, the villagers went looking for the boy who hadn’t returned with their sheep. When they went up the hill, they found him (5)________ as all the sheep were dead. ![](https://cdn.testbook.com/resources/lms_creative_elements/additional-information-image.png) Additional Information • Driving: "Driving" implies using a vehicle, which is unlikely and not suitable for reaching someone on a hillside urgently. • Walking down: "Walking down" suggests movement away from the hill, contrary to the necessary action of going towards the boy in need. • Running down: "Running down" again indicates descending the hill, which does not align with the context where villagers need to climb up to reach the boy.

questions.question: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. Once, there was a boy who became bored when he watched over the village sheep (1)________ on the hillside. To entertain himself, he sang out, “Wolf! Wolf! The wolf is chasing the sheep!” When the villagers heard the cry, they came (2)________ the hill to drive the wolf away. But, when they arrived, they saw no wolf. The boy was amused when seeing their angry faces. “Don’t scream wolf, boy,” warned the villagers, “when there is no wolf!” They angrily went back down the hill. Later, the shepherd boy cried out once again, “Wolf! Wolf! The wolf is chasing the sheep!” When the villagers came, they saw there was no wolf, they said strictly, “Save your (3)________ cry for when there is really a wolf! Don’t cry ‘wolf’ when there is no wolf!” Later, the boy saw a real wolf sneaking around his flock. Alarmed, he jumped on his feet and cried out as loud as he could, “Wolf! Wolf!” But the villagers thought he was (4)________ them again, and so they didn’t come to help. At sunset, the villagers went looking for the boy who hadn’t returned with their sheep. When they went up the hill, they found him (5)________ as all the sheep were dead. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 3.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is 'frightened'. ![](https://cdn.testbook.com/resources/lms_creative_elements/key-point-image.png) Key Points • The context involves a boy repeatedly crying "Wolf!" falsely and being warned to save his cry for genuine danger. • The appropriate adjective describes the seriousness and urgency for a true emergency. • "Frightened" (डरे हुए) aligns with the genuine feeling of fear that should accompany a real wolf sighting. • Other options ("laughable" (हास्यास्पद), "funny" (मजेदार), "muffled" (दबा हुआ)) do not convey the seriousness needed for a warning about a dangerous situation. Therefore, the correct answer is 'Option 1'. Complete Passage: Once, there was a boy who became bored when he watched over the village sheep grazing on the hillside. To entertain himself, he sang out, “Wolf! Wolf! The wolf is chasing the sheep!” When the villagers heard the cry, they came running up the hill to drive the wolf away. But, when they arrived, they saw no wolf. The boy was amused when seeing their angry faces. “Don’t scream wolf, boy,” warned the villagers, “when there is no wolf!” They angrily went back down the hill. Later, the shepherd boy cried out once again, “Wolf! Wolf! The wolf is chasing the sheep!” When the villagers came, they saw there was no wolf, they said strictly, “Save your frightened cry for when there is really a wolf! Don’t cry ‘wolf’ when there is no wolf!” Later, the boy saw a real wolf sneaking around his flock. Alarmed, he jumped on his feet and cried out as loud as he could, “Wolf! Wolf!” But the villagers thought he was (4)________ them again, and so they didn’t come to help. At sunset, the villagers went looking for the boy who hadn’t returned with their sheep. When they went up the hill, they found him (5)________ as all the sheep were dead. ![](https://cdn.testbook.com/resources/lms_creative_elements/additional-information-image.png) Additional Information • Laughable (हास्यास्पद): "Laughable" (हास्यास्पद) implies something that is ridiculous or amusing, which is inappropriate for a serious warning about danger. • Funny (मजेदार): Similar to "laughable," (हास्यास्पद), "funny" (मजेदार) suggests humor or amusement rather than the gravity of a real threat. • Muffled (दबा हुआ): "Muffled" (दबा हुआ) means softened or muted, which doesn't convey the appropriate urgency and emotion for a warning about a wolf.

questions.question: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. Once, there was a boy who became bored when he watched over the village sheep (1)________ on the hillside. To entertain himself, he sang out, “Wolf! Wolf! The wolf is chasing the sheep!” When the villagers heard the cry, they came (2)________ the hill to drive the wolf away. But, when they arrived, they saw no wolf. The boy was amused when seeing their angry faces. “Don’t scream wolf, boy,” warned the villagers, “when there is no wolf!” They angrily went back down the hill. Later, the shepherd boy cried out once again, “Wolf! Wolf! The wolf is chasing the sheep!” When the villagers came, they saw there was no wolf, they said strictly, “Save your (3)________ cry for when there is really a wolf! Don’t cry ‘wolf’ when there is no wolf!” Later, the boy saw a real wolf sneaking around his flock. Alarmed, he jumped on his feet and cried out as loud as he could, “Wolf! Wolf!” But the villagers thought he was (4)________ them again, and so they didn’t come to help. At sunset, the villagers went looking for the boy who hadn’t returned with their sheep. When they went up the hill, they found him (5)________ as all the sheep were dead. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 4.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is 'fooling'. ![](https://cdn.testbook.com/resources/lms_creative_elements/key-point-image.png) Key Points • The context of the passage is about a boy who repeatedly deceives the villagers by making false claims of a wolf attacking the sheep. • The villagers' reaction indicates they believe the boy is not telling the truth and is playing a trick on them. • The word "fooling" (धोखा देना) means deceiving or tricking someone, which aligns with the boy’s actions in the story. • Therefore, "fooling" (धोखा देना) fits naturally into the sentence structure and conveys the intended meaning that the villagers thought the boy was deceiving them again. Therefore, the correct answer is- ‘Option 1'. Complete Passage: Once, there was a boy who became bored when he watched over the village sheep grazing on the hillside. To entertain himself, he sang out, “Wolf! Wolf! The wolf is chasing the sheep!” When the villagers heard the cry, they came running up the hill to drive the wolf away. But, when they arrived, they saw no wolf. The boy was amused when seeing their angry faces. “Don’t scream wolf, boy,” warned the villagers, “when there is no wolf!” They angrily went back down the hill. Later, the shepherd boy cried out once again, “Wolf! Wolf! The wolf is chasing the sheep!” When the villagers came, they saw there was no wolf, they said strictly, “Save your frightened cry for when there is really a wolf! Don’t cry ‘wolf’ when there is no wolf!” Later, the boy saw a real wolf sneaking around his flock. Alarmed, he jumped on his feet and cried out as loud as he could, “Wolf! Wolf!” But the villagers thought he was fooling them again, and so they didn’t come to help. At sunset, the villagers went looking for the boy who hadn’t returned with their sheep. When they went up the hill, they found him (5)________ as all the sheep were dead. ![](https://cdn.testbook.com/resources/lms_creative_elements/additional-information-image.png) Additional Information • questioning (सवाल करना): This word implies doubt or inquiry, which doesn't fit the context where the villagers believe the boy's intentions are deceptive rather than uncertain. • fighting (लड़ाई करना): This suggests a conflict or struggle, which doesn't align with the scenario where the villagers stay away because they think the boy is lying. • seeing (देखना): This implies observing or noticing, which does not make sense in the context suggesting the villagers believe the boy is tricking them.

questions.question: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. Once, there was a boy who became bored when he watched over the village sheep (1)________ on the hillside. To entertain himself, he sang out, “Wolf! Wolf! The wolf is chasing the sheep!” When the villagers heard the cry, they came (2)________ the hill to drive the wolf away. But, when they arrived, they saw no wolf. The boy was amused when seeing their angry faces. “Don’t scream wolf, boy,” warned the villagers, “when there is no wolf!” They angrily went back down the hill. Later, the shepherd boy cried out once again, “Wolf! Wolf! The wolf is chasing the sheep!” When the villagers came, they saw there was no wolf, they said strictly, “Save your (3)________ cry for when there is really a wolf! Don’t cry ‘wolf’ when there is no wolf!” Later, the boy saw a real wolf sneaking around his flock. Alarmed, he jumped on his feet and cried out as loud as he could, “Wolf! Wolf!” But the villagers thought he was (4)________ them again, and so they didn’t come to help. At sunset, the villagers went looking for the boy who hadn’t returned with their sheep. When they went up the hill, they found him (5)________ as all the sheep were dead. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 5.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is 'weeping'. ![](https://cdn.testbook.com/resources/lms_creative_elements/key-point-image.png) Key Points • The context of the sentence involves a reaction to an event where the boy is genuinely in distress because a wolf is attacking the sheep. • The appropriate emotional response in such a distressing and urgent situation would be sadness or fear, not joy or celebration. • The word "weeping" (रोना) means crying, which matches the boy's state of despair upon realizing the gravity of the situation. • Hence, "weeping" (रोना) fits naturally into the sentence and conveys the intended distress and urgency experienced by the boy. Therefore, the correct answer is- ‘Option 4'. Complete Passage: Once, there was a boy who became bored when he watched over the village sheep grazing on the hillside. To entertain himself, he sang out, “Wolf! Wolf! The wolf is chasing the sheep!” When the villagers heard the cry, they came running up the hill to drive the wolf away. But, when they arrived, they saw no wolf. The boy was amused when seeing their angry faces. “Don’t scream wolf, boy,” warned the villagers, “when there is no wolf!” They angrily went back down the hill. Later, the shepherd boy cried out once again, “Wolf! Wolf! The wolf is chasing the sheep!” When the villagers came, they saw there was no wolf, they said strictly, “Save your frightened cry for when there is really a wolf! Don’t cry ‘wolf’ when there is no wolf!” Later, the boy saw a real wolf sneaking around his flock. Alarmed, he jumped on his feet and cried out as loud as he could, “Wolf! Wolf!” But the villagers thought he was fooling them again, and so they didn’t come to help. At sunset, the villagers went looking for the boy who hadn’t returned with their sheep. When they went up the hill, they found him weeping as all the sheep were dead. ![](https://cdn.testbook.com/resources/lms_creative_elements/additional-information-image.png) Additional Information • singing (गाना): This implies a joyous activity, which is inappropriate for a situation where the boy is in genuine trouble. • happy (खुश): This suggests a state of joy, which does not fit the context where the boy is in distress due to the wolf attack. • dancing (नृत्य करना): This also indicates celebration or happiness, which contradicts the boy’s dire situation.
